pieceInfo.tuong: The General (King) moves one step orthogonally — up, down, left, or right. It must stay inside the 3×3 palace. If both Generals face each other on the same column with no pieces between them, the move is illegal ("flying generals" rule).
pieceInfo.si: The Advisor moves one step diagonally. It must stay inside the 3×3 palace, so it only has 5 possible positions.
pieceInfo.tuongVoi: The Elephant moves exactly 2 steps diagonally, forming a wide diagonal jump. It cannot cross the river (stays on its own half). If the intermediate diagonal square is occupied, the move is blocked ("eye block").
pieceInfo.xe: The Chariot (Rook) moves any number of squares orthogonally — up, down, left, or right — until blocked. It captures by landing on an enemy piece. The most powerful piece.
pieceInfo.phao: The Cannon moves like the Chariot for non-capture moves (sliding orthogonally). To capture, it must jump over exactly one piece (the "screen") and land on an enemy piece behind it.
pieceInfo.ma: The Horse (Knight) moves in an L-shape: one step orthogonally, then one step diagonally away. If the adjacent orthogonal square is occupied, that direction is blocked ("leg block").
pieceInfo.tot: The Soldier (Pawn) moves one step forward before crossing the river. After crossing the river, it can also move one step sideways (left or right). It never moves backward.
